question:
Will "Zootopia 2" 5-Day Opening Box Office be greater than 157m?

Description: This market will resolve according to how much "Zootopia 2" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 5-day opening (November 26 - November 30) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 5-day opening, regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by December 7, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

Resolution source: https://www.the-numbers.com/

evidences.title: ‘Zootopia 2’ Arrives in... - Beyond Hollywood Updates
evidences.content: ## Beyond Hollywood Updates's post ### Beyond Hollywood Updates 27 October 2025 · ‘Zootopia 2’ Arrives in Theaters on November 26, 2025 — Sequel Set for Big Opening Disney’s highly anticipated sequel, Zootopia 2, will hit theaters in exactly one month, on November 26, 2025. • The film reunites bunny cop Judy Hopps (voiced by Ginnifer Goodwin) and fox partner Nick Wilde (voiced by Jason Bateman) in a new mystery set within the mammal city of Zootopia. • Early box office predictions suggest a strong opening weekend of $95 million–$110 million, and a robust five‐day haul of $140 million–$160 million. Why This Release Is Significant [...] Why This Release Is Significant • The original Zootopia (2016) was a major success, both critically and financially: crossing $1 billion globally and earning the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature. • With the nine year gap between films, Disney appears to be positioning Zootopia 2 as one of the major holiday animated tentpoles of 2025. • The sequel’s arrival during a high profile theatrical window offers the franchise a strong platform to drive both domestic and international box office. With just one month to go before Zootopia 2 premieres, a...

evidences.title: Zootopia 2 Box Office Projections at $150 Million Over Thanksgiving
evidences.content: Senior Film and Media Reporter #### Latest GREAT MYSTERY -- Walt Disney Animation Studios’ “Zootopia 2” welcomes back to the big screen rookie cops Judy Hopps (voice of Ginnifer Goodwin) and Nick Wilde (voice of Jason Bateman). When a snake called Gary De’Snake (voice of Ke Huy Quan) arrives on the scene, he kicks off a great mystery—but Nick and Judy are on the case. From the Oscar-winning team of Disney Animation chief creative officer Jared Bush and Byron Howard (directors) and Yvett Merino (producer), “Zootopia 2” releases in theaters Nov. 26. © 2025 Disney Enterprises, Inc. All Rights Reserved. Get ready for a box office feast. [...] On paper, this Thanksgiving lineup is reminiscent of last year’s offerings, which included a Disney animated sequel (“Moana 2”) and a “Wicked” installment. Those two films, along with the big-budget “Gladiator II,” helped to cement a Turkey Day weekend record with $422 million in grosses across all releases. Although the 2025 holiday looks promising for exhibitors, this crop of films might not surpass the 2024 benchmark, as there’s no “Gladiator”-esque tentpole to cater to teenage boys and men. But this weekend could contend for the second-larg...

evidences.title: Can 'Zootopia 2,' 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' Save 2025 Box Office?
evidences.content: Projections for the year were revised down to $9 billion, which would rank just above 2024 ($8.7 billion), slightly behind 2023 (a post-COVID record $9.04 billion) and way behind pre-pandemic heights of $10.5 billion to $11 billion. For a while, exhibitors were dealing with lighter release calendars. Now box office watchers believe the lack of recovery has less to do with quantity. “If something is working or not, it’s more quality driven,” says Barrington Research analyst Patrick Sholl. [...] Back in January, analysts predicted the North American box office would enjoy the best year since COVID with $9.3 billion to $9.5 billion. Audiences didn’t get the memo. “Lilo & Stitch” ($1.04 billion), “A Minecraft Movie” ($957 million) and “Jurassic World Rebirth” ($868 million) were certified blockbusters. “F1: The Movie” ($631 million), “Sinners” ($367 million), and “Weapons” ($268 million) became sleeper hits. But those wins weren’t enough to offset several big-budget blunders, including “Captain America: Brave New World” ($415 million against a $180 million budget), “Tron: Ares” ($141 million against a $180 million budget) and “Mission: Impossible – The Final Reckoning” ($598 million...

evidences.title: Can ZOOTOPIA 2 Break MOANA 2's Thanksgiving Weekend Records?
evidences.content: Despite ample surface-level similarities between _Moana 2_ and _Zootopia 2_—both are sequels to 2016 Disney animated hits coming out over Thanksgiving weekend—our forecasting doesn’t have the latter film reaching $200M over the five-day holiday as _Moana 2_ did, though predictions may budge upwards as we get closer to release. It’s worth noting that the first _Zootopia_ outgrossed the first _Moana_ by a substantial amount, pulling in $341.2M at the domestic box office to the latter’s $248.7M. Another hopeful factoid is that, among the nine Disney animated sequels (both Pixar and Walt Disney Animation Studios) to hit theaters over the last decade, all but two (_Cars 3_ and _Lightyear_) grossed more than their predecessors. [...] Disney will try to follow up the success of its Thanksgiving 2024 release of _Moana 2_ with another animated sequel, as the _Zootopia_ universe returns to theaters over the holiday weekend. Last year’s combined Thanksgiving weekend five-day box office—$382.5M, with 75% of showtimes coming from the top three films (_Moana 2,_ _Wicked_, _Gladiator II_)—was the biggest ever for the holiday, just as _Moana 2_‘s debut frame broke a slew of individual records,...

evidences.title: “You Are Not Responsible for Their Choices, Elsa”: The Lion King (2019), Frozen II (2019) and the Theatre of Photorealist Achievement | animationstudies 2.0
evidences.content: In early January 2020, Variety reported that Walt Disney’s feature film Frozen II (Jennifer Lee & Chris Buck, 2019) was now “officially the highest-grossing animated movie in history” (Rubin, 2020). In what was emphatically described as a “box-office achievement fit for royalty,” Rebecca Rubin declared: “The sequel has generated $1.325 billion at the global box office, pushing past Frozen ($1.281 million) and Incredibles 2 ($1.243 million) to secure the top spot.” Many other news outlets staked similar claims, identifying not only how Frozen II had achieved the biggest opening weekend for an animated film during its initial theatrical release – earning $358 million (£277 million) in its first three days in 37 countries including the US, China, and the UK – but that the sequel had [...] A three-dimensional computer-animated film, Frozen II was produced as the 58th entry into Walt Disney’s Feature Animation canon, and the last in a decade that has seen the studio release eight other animated features under the WDAS banner: Tangled (Nathan Greno & Byron Howard, 2010), Winnie the Pooh (Stephen J. Anderson & Don Hall, 2011), Wreck-It Ralph (Rich Moore, 2012), Frozen (Chis Buck & Jenn...

evidences.title: 'Moana 2' Box Office: Tracking Puts Disney Sequel On Par With Disney Originals
evidences.content: If that holds, it’ll be the biggest Thanksgiving domestic debut ever (sans inflation). It will also be the second or third biggest Wed-Sun Thanksgiving weekend gross alongside The Hunger Games: Catching Fire ($100 million in 2013 following a $158 million Fri-Sun debut) and Frozen II ($125 million in 2019 after a $130 million Fri-Sun launch). The Outside Scoop is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. [...] Yes, this would be a “comeback” after Ecanto ($41 million in 2021 with a mere 31-day pre-Disney+ window), Strange World ($19 million in 2022 likewise) and Wish ($32 million in 2023 partially because it was the worst Walt Disney animated movie since Chicken Little in 2005). Assuming Moana 2doesn’t blow this initial tracking out of the water (see - Inside Out 2, although Quarum pegged Deadpool & Wolverine as a $200 million-plus opener from the start), inflation-adjusted comparisons tell a slightly more complicated story. Frozen II’s $125 million \second weekend\ Wed-Sun Thanksgiving gross would be around $147 million adjusted for inflation. [...] If Ralph Breaks the Internet was “fine for a Disney to...

evidences.title: [PDF] 2025-09-29 - Zootopia 2 - Final Draft - LOCKED - Disney.com
evidences.content: Gary smiles at Hopps, oddly vulnerable, but just as Hopps starts to believe him - CLONK! Out of nowhere, Nick arrives, whacking Gary with a frying pan, knocking him out. Gary collapses and his coils release Lynxley and the JOURNAL, which falls to the ground. NICK (out of breath) Woo! I am here! (to Lynxley, re: Judy) Hopps and Wilde. Dream team. We got him. Or her. Reptiles. It’s like who knows. Hopps looks at Nick like what did you do?! Judy goes to check on Gary, who is out cold. Kitty and Cattrick barge in. KITTY The cops are right behind us. MILTON LYNXLEY (taking a dark turn) Kill the snake, we’ll burn the journal-- if he wants it, it’s dangerous. (to Judy and Nick) And you two will file a report that the he attacked us and you will keep your mouths shut. Step aside. [...] Zootopia 2 - Final Draft - 9-29-25 - 59. In the center of the room is a table with evidence strung together... old newspaper clippings... one says “REPTILES AND MAMMALS, TOO DIFFERENT FOR ONE CITY?” “EBENEZER LYNXLEY’S PLAN FOR ALL ANIMALS MARRED BY SNAKE ATTACK” “WHY DID THE SNAKE DO IT?” “TUNDRATOWN EXPANDED” another says “TUNDRATOWN EXPANDED” another says “CITY APPROVES MORE TUNDRATOWN” - “NO REPTILES,...

evidences.title: Disney’s upcoming live-action Moana is... - Beyond Toon World
evidences.content: ## Beyond Toon World's Post ### Beyond Toon World June 19 at 1:30 AM · Disney’s upcoming live-action Moana is currently tracking for an estimated $85 million opening weekend at the North American box office. If the projection holds, it would debut more than $50 million behind the $139.7 million three-day opening of Moana 2 in 2024, highlighting the animated sequel’s record-breaking success. While early tracking offers an initial snapshot of audience interest, box office projections can change as release day approaches. #Moana #Disney #LiveActionMoana #Moana2 #BoxOffice #MovieNews May be an image of text that says 'Disnep MTANA BEYOND OON WORLD DISNEY'S LIVE- LIVE-ACTION MOANA TRACKING FOR $85M OPENING WEEKEND.'
